text:
An airports union in Paris has announced it will hold strike action on July 26, the opening day of the city'sOlympic Games.

The union – Force Ouvrière–is demanding more pay concessions as employees are "dissatisfied"with negotiations so far.

The walkout will take place from 5am on Friday July 26 to 7am on Saturday July 27.

This comes as air traffic at the Paris-Charles-de-Gaulle, Orly and Beauvais airports will be suspended for the opening ceremony on Friday.

A deal had been reached between the airport staff and their employers last week, with the union announcing that the strike action was now off.

Don't miss:ISIS is earmarking two major events in France for 'attack' as security ramps up



At the time, Rachid Eddaidj, secretary general of the CFDT union's branch at Paris Airports (ADP), said: "We're lifting the strike warning, there's a majority deal."

Charles de Gaulle is expecting peaks of 300,000 travellers passing through in a single day ahead of the Olympic Games.

As France prepares to host the games in its capital, it has had to deal with a number of logistical and security concerns prior to the event.

The opening ceremony, and many events, will be held in the River Seine, which has been subject to a huge cleaning operation to make sure the water is safe.

Amid the threat of terror attacks, around 35,000 police officers will be deployed in Paris each day during the Games while 45,000 will be on duty for the opening ceremony.

Don't miss:Nightmare for Macron as Paris Olympics in 'crisis' after new strikes announced

Don't miss:Olympics 2024 star tests out 'anti-sex beds' and posts video on social media

Don't miss:Paris Olympics chaos as half a million tickets still unsold ahead of Games



Paris will also have 10,000 soldiers and 1,800 police officers from abroad helping with security.

Nicolas Nordman, Deputy Paris Mayor in charge of security, toldCNNthat "strictly controlled" security zones have already been imposed.

He added: “These controls must ensure that no ill-intentioned people enter the area. There is also the need for controls on the day of the ceremony.

“This means that when people come to the site, we have to make sure that there are no security risks.

“What we want is for people to enjoy the Olympic Games. One of the conditions for them to enjoy them is to be safe. But we also want them to be able to move around in order to enjoy them.”

title: Olympic Games 'crisis' as Paris airports to be hit by strikes on first day | World | News | Express.co.uk
author: Maria Ortega, Charlie Bradley
description: Airport staff will go on strike after pay negotiations broke down ahead of the Paris Olympic Games.
